jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Cold open with Homer's signature catchphrase
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical but not original at this point in the series

jokes.text: Same old garbage
jokes.setup: Homer asks family to guess what's different about dinner
jokes.punchline: Bart immediately deflates Homer's enthusiasm with brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart cynicism delivered with perfect timing to deflate Homer's excitement

jokes.text: This guy's always on!
jokes.setup: Family discussion about genetically modified vegetables
jokes.punchline: Homer's non-sequitur comment treating Bart like a TV character
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment showing Homer's obliviousness but not particularly clever

jokes.text: This corn doesn't look so big. / That's baby corn. / What?
jokes.setup: Bart commenting on corn size after talk of giant genetically modified vegetables
jokes.punchline: Lisa's matter-of-fact correction revealing Bart's confusion
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection joke playing on expectations vs reality, well-structured

jokes.text: They're all grown by the vegetable division of Union Carbide
jokes.setup: Homer reassuring family about genetically modified vegetables being safe
jokes.punchline: References Union Carbide, company responsible for Bhopal disaster
jokes.explanation: Dark satirical reference that works on multiple levels - Homer's ignorance and corporate irresponsibility

jokes.text: Mom, my potato is eating a carrot!
jokes.punchline: Vegetables have become so mutated they're carnivorous
jokes.explanation: Escalates the GM food absurdity to logical extreme with vivid visual comedy

jokes.text: I've tried heckling them. I've tried jeckling them.
jokes.setup: Marge explaining her attempts to scare away crows
jokes.punchline: Made-up word 'jeckling' as escalation from heckling
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - earnest problem-solving with invented vocabulary that sounds logical

jokes.text: "... in God, the Father Almighty Creator of heaven and earth... " Shoo! Shoo!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to scare crows while reciting the Apostles' Creed
jokes.punchline: Mixing sacred prayer with mundane crow-shooing
jokes.explanation: Classic Marge trying to multitask religious devotion with practical problems

jokes.text: "Heaven and earth... " Where were we? Now I'm lost.
jokes.setup: Marge interrupted herself mixing prayer with crow-shooing
jokes.punchline: Lost track of both the prayer and her original purpose
jokes.explanation: Natural follow-up showing Marge's confusion from her own multitasking

jokes.text: Yeah, I did it! Marge is in the house!
jokes.setup: Marge celebrating victory over the crows
jokes.punchline: Using hip-hop celebration language completely out of character
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy - prim Marge using street slang is perfectly absurd

jokes.text: I will be soon. Because it needs some cleaning!
jokes.setup: Marge declaring 'Marge is in the house!'
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to domestic concerns about cleaning
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of her brief hip moment back to pure Marge domesticity

jokes.text: I'll regroup at Red Lobster.
jokes.punchline: Strategic retreat to chain restaurant as if it's a military operation
jokes.explanation: Homer treating domestic failure like military campaign, with Red Lobster as base camp

jokes.text: You ain't pretty no more!
jokes.punchline: Trash-talking an inanimate object
jokes.explanation: Standard Homer physical comedy with object, moderately funny

jokes.text: This ain't no crow bar! This is a crow bar! See? They got the little stools and everything.
jokes.setup: Moe complaining about crows in his bar
jokes.punchline: Reveals actual crow bar with tiny furniture for crows
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual pun that works on multiple levels with actual tiny crow furniture

jokes.text: That's Russell Crow, Cameron Crow, Crow Diddley, Hume Crow-nyn, Gregory Peck.
jokes.setup: Moe identifying the crows at his crow bar
jokes.punchline: Series of crow puns on celebrity names, ending with Gregory Peck (bird reference)
jokes.explanation: Clever escalating wordplay with good payoff in Gregory Peck (bird name)

jokes.text: It's a murder, honey. A group of crows is called a murder.
jokes.punchline: Homer pedantically correcting with ominous collective noun
jokes.explanation: Educational humor with slight menace, though the collective noun joke is fairly common

jokes.text: Drop it in! Don't cram it in! Forget it! I'll do it myself.
jokes.setup: Homer directing crows in some task
jokes.punchline: Frustrated micromanaging of wild birds
jokes.explanation: Homer's control-freak nature applied to impossible situation of directing animals

jokes.text: That is not a worm!
jokes.punchline: Homer clarifying what body part they're attacking
jokes.explanation: Crude visual gag relying on anatomy humor, gets reaction but not sophisticated

jokes.text: They weren't trying to blind you. They were just trying to drink your sweet, sweet eye juices.
jokes.punchline: Doctor's disturbing but matter-of-fact explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly delivered dark humor with medical authority making it more disturbing

jokes.text: Does it go in the butt?
jokes.punchline: Homer's immediate crude concern
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer reaction - crude but perfectly in character medical anxiety

jokes.text: Prescription pot. Texas THC.
jokes.punchline: Alliterative drug nicknames
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay on marijuana terminology, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Homer, for your eyes, the best tonic is chronic.
jokes.punchline: Doctor's rhyming medical advice
jokes.explanation: Clean rhyming wordplay that works as medical advice and drug slang

jokes.text: Uh-oh. Crotch the weed, man.
jokes.punchline: Otto's malapropism for 'stash the drugs'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Otto line - stoner malapropism that's both crude and somehow logical

jokes.text: For me, the '60s ended that day in 1978.
jokes.punchline: Historically incorrect end date for the 1960s
jokes.explanation: Clever historical joke playing on generational clichés about when the '60s really ended

jokes.text: But isn't marijuana, or "dope," illegal? / Only for those who enjoy it.
jokes.setup: Homer's innocent question about marijuana legality
jokes.punchline: Doctor's cynical distinction about medical vs recreational use
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical observation about medical marijuana laws and their arbitrary nature

jokes.text: Do you want the wizard or the skull?
jokes.punchline: Offering decorative options for medical equipment
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical professionalism applied to drug paraphernalia aesthetics

jokes.text: "Caution, objects may appear more edible than they actually are."
jokes.punchline: Warning label parody about marijuana munchies
jokes.explanation: Clever parody of side mirror warnings applied to drug side effects

jokes.text: They burned down the gambling house / It died with an awful sound
jokes.setup: Homer improvising 'Smoke on the Water' lyrics
jokes.punchline: Replacing rock lyrics with Simpsons-appropriate content
jokes.explanation: Mild parody of famous rock song with family-friendly alternative lyrics

jokes.text: I am hungry for a candy bar / I think I'll eat a Mounds
jokes.setup: Homer continuing improvised song while high
jokes.punchline: Mundane munchies observation in rock song format
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between epic rock format and trivial stoner concerns

jokes.text: And now I'm guilty of possession! I could go to prison unless I testify against you.
jokes.punchline: Immediate leap to worst-case legal scenario
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge anxiety escalation to absurd legal consequences

jokes.text: I could walk up to the president and blow smoke in his stupid, monkey face! And he'd just have to sit there grooving on it.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining the legality of his marijuana use
jokes.punchline: Fantasizing about disrespecting the president with impunity
jokes.explanation: Homer's anarchic fantasy with good visual and political irreverence

jokes.text: Marge, I just realized I am the "ow" in the word "now."
jokes.punchline: Profound-sounding but nonsensical word observation
jokes.explanation: Excellent stoner pseudo-philosophy that sounds deep but is meaningless

jokes.text: Hey, it's me. I've got Marge on the other line and she is totally bumming me out.
jokes.punchline: Calling someone else to complain about his wife
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's stoned social obliviousness

jokes.text: Daddy's special medicine, which you must never use because it will ruin your life, lets Daddy see and hear magical things you will never experience.
jokes.punchline: Simultaneously warning against and promoting drug use
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental mixed message delivered with Homer's oblivious honesty

jokes.text: Dad, it's 1:00 a.m. and I'm out of saliva!
jokes.setup: Homer keeping Lisa up playing saxophone
jokes.punchline: Lisa's specific physical complaint about overplaying
jokes.explanation: Specific musical detail that shows exhaustion in relatable musician terms

jokes.text: This dude does the best Flanders! You got the mustache and the "diddily!"
jokes.punchline: Homer treating real Flanders like an impersonator
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke where character doesn't recognize his own neighbor due to drugs

jokes.text: "Could Jesus microwave a burrito so hot that he himself could not eat it?"
jokes.setup: Homer asking Ned a theological question
jokes.punchline: Stoner version of omnipotence paradox
jokes.explanation: Brilliant philosophical paradox updated for modern stoner context - highly quotable

jokes.text: Well, sir, of course he could. But then again, wow, as melon-scratchers go, that's a honey-doodle.
jokes.punchline: Ned's folksy language trying to handle deep philosophy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ned response - earnest attempt at theology in his characteristic folksy style

jokes.text: All right, Simpson, you're under arrest, see? / I said Wiggum!
jokes.punchline: Homer's dissatisfaction with the generic cop voice
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier request, shows Homer's stoned perfectionism about impressions

jokes.text: "And so, potential investors, I hope you don't think our IPO is an IPU."
jokes.punchline: IPO/IPU pun (Initial Public Offering/Initial Public Urination)
jokes.explanation: Mild business pun that's somewhat clever but relies on crude humor

jokes.text: I told you, I pissed it away! Don't make that face. / Yes, that's the one.
jokes.punchline: Burns recognizing Smithers' disapproving expression
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic showing Burns' awareness of Smithers' judgment

jokes.text: Oops! I thought this was the can, man.
jokes.setup: Homer entering Burns' office while high
jokes.punchline: Mistaking office for bathroom
jokes.explanation: Standard stoner confusion joke, gets mild laugh but predictable

jokes.text: Working hard or hardly working?
jokes.punchline: Ancient workplace cliché delivered earnestly
jokes.explanation: Homer's oblivious use of tired workplace humor as if it's fresh

jokes.text: No, Smithers. I would've said anything to get your stem cells.
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual admission of medical exploitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns line - casual evil delivered with corporate efficiency

jokes.text: You're covered with a very fine fuzz.
jokes.punchline: Homer's stoned observation about Burns' appearance
jokes.explanation: Mild stoner observation, slightly unsettling but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I just got promoted, and it's all thanks to "Yes-I-cannabis!"
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his workplace success to Marge
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Yes we can' with marijuana reference
jokes.explanation: Solid political slogan pun adapted for marijuana context

jokes.text: You mutton heads! This is why we can never hold down a job.
jokes.setup: Moe addressing the Three Stooges working for Homer
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about Stooges' employment history
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about Three Stooges' classic incompetence applied to modern employment

jokes.text: Hey, "Shemp" is "hemp" spelled backwards. / And "Otto" is "Otto" backwards!
jokes.punchline: Otto's proud but pointless palindrome observation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from clever observation to proudly stupid one - perfect Otto

jokes.text: Marge, I'm gonna need 10,000 veggie burritos! / No guac in mine.
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous scale followed by Otto's personal preference
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from grand political gesture to mundane food preference

jokes.text: Dude, your mom is hot.
jokes.punchline: Otto's inappropriate comment to Homer
jokes.explanation: Standard stoner inappropriate comment, perfectly in character for Otto

jokes.text: Whoa! This guy is seriously ill. / My doctor never told me that. I had to hear it from Phish.
jokes.punchline: Homer learning about his condition from rock band instead of doctor
jokes.explanation: Good satire of both medical communication and stoner culture authority

jokes.text: My doctor never told me that. I had to hear it from Phish.
jokes.punchline: Homer trusting rock band over doctor
jokes.explanation: Great observation about Homer's misplaced trust in authority figures

jokes.text: And now, a man who's a real pothead. I'm sorry. 'Pothead.'
jokes.punchline: Self-correction doesn't improve the pun
jokes.explanation: Self-aware bad pun that acknowledges its own weakness

jokes.text: Whether you suffer from glaucoma or you just rented The Matrix, medical marijuana can make things fabulous, medically.
jokes.punchline: Equating serious medical conditions with movie rental
jokes.explanation: Satirizes loose medical marijuana justifications and cultural references

jokes.text: Oh, man! They already voted! And we lost! / Oh, man! I can't believe we spaced on the date!
jokes.punchline: Using 'spaced' pun while being literally spaced out
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'spaced' that works literally and figuratively

jokes.text: Well, we can't just stand here staring at our hands, although... Wow!
jokes.punchline: Immediately getting distracted by stoner hand fascination
jokes.explanation: Classic stoner cliché executed with perfect timing and character work

jokes.text: They call them fingers, but I never see them fing. Oh, there they go.
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-profound observation about finger etymology with movement
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd stoner observation with perfect delivery and timing

jokes.text: I could've smoked that pot and worn that hair.
jokes.setup: Watching marijuana and barber hair being burned
jokes.punchline: Homer's specific regret about waste
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's practical regret over abstract loss

jokes.text: You know, I'm not really sure what I want.
jokes.setup: Lisa wanting her old dad back
jokes.punchline: Honest admission of conflicted feelings
jokes.explanation: Rare moment of Lisa uncertainty that humanizes her usual certainty

jokes.text: As long as you're doing things for me, would you tie up your bathrobe when you walk around the house? / Never!
jokes.setup: Bart making additional request of Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's defiant refusal of basic decency
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - willing to give up drugs but not basic dignity

jokes.text: Remember when I dropped my keys and you thought the phone was ringing? / Yeah. / Get out.
jokes.punchline: Homer's cold dismissal after brief nostalgic moment
jokes.explanation: Good comedy timing - brief sentiment immediately cut off by harsh reality

jokes.text: Going cold turkey isn't as delicious as it sounds.
jokes.punchline: Homer's literal interpretation of the idiom
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay on common idiom, very Homer-esque literal thinking

jokes.text: Yeah. But at the planning party, I got alcohol poisoning. I nearly died. / I was already making excuses not to go to your funeral.
jokes.punchline: Homer's selfish honesty about friend's near-death
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest friendship moment showing Homer's casual selfishness

jokes.text: My sperm count is up, and I'm able to recognize simple shapes and patterns. / Dad, you just said that three minutes ago.
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrating memory problems
jokes.explanation: Excellent ironic structure - claiming improved cognition while demonstrating the opposite

jokes.text: Unregardless, I will no longer be a slave to this!
jokes.setup: Homer making declaration about giving up drugs
jokes.punchline: Using made-up word 'unregardless' while claiming mental clarity
jokes.explanation: Homer's malapropism perfectly undermines his attempt at eloquent sobriety

jokes.text: I'll either tickle your ribs or feed them to my dogs!
jokes.setup: Burns demanding Homer laugh at his jokes
jokes.punchline: Threatening literal rib removal via wordplay
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Burns threat using 'tickle your ribs' literally - perfectly evil wordplay

jokes.text: What do you take me for? Lorenzo de' Medici?
jokes.setup: Homer suggesting extra cheese on pizza
jokes.punchline: Burns treating pizza topping as extravagant luxury
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns reference comparing pizza cheese to Renaissance patronage excess

jokes.text: You know, Louise Brooks, the silent star of Lulu.
jokes.punchline: Explaining his own joke to confused audience
jokes.explanation: Burns' typical dated cultural references falling flat

jokes.text: This stuff can make anything funny, even that show that follows Friends.
jokes.punchline: Reference to show in Friends' time slot (possibly The Simpsons itself)
jokes.explanation: Possible self-referential joke about The Simpsons following Friends on Fox

jokes.text: This suit used to belong to Judy Garland. We could sing a song if you don't mind being Mickey Rooney.
jokes.punchline: Reference to classic Hollywood musical partnership
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate reference showing Smithers' theatrical knowledge and gay coding

jokes.text: You mean that guy on 60 Minutes who yells all the time?
jokes.punchline: Homer confusing with Andy Rooney from 60 Minutes
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer cultural confusion between two different Rooneys

jokes.text: Sixty minutes? Oh, my God! Mr. Burns has been in the tub for an hour!
jokes.punchline: Smithers realizing time passage and Burns danger
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from casual conversation to crisis realization

jokes.text: Oh, Mr. Burns! You were too beautiful for this world.
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate eulogy for evil old man
jokes.explanation: Perfect Smithers delusion about Burns' character even in death

jokes.text: Oh, man. Oh, man. We killed Mr. Burns! / Mr. Burns is gonna be so mad!
jokes.punchline: Worrying about dead Burns being angry
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - fearing consequences from person who can no longer give them

jokes.text: Michael Eisner's been dead for five years. Ted Turner's just a hologram.
jokes.setup: Smithers saying they can carry on without Burns
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation about other media moguls
jokes.explanation: Dark satirical take on media industry suggesting widespread executive death cover-ups

jokes.text: "It depends on what the meaning of 'was' is, jerk!"
jokes.punchline: Adding 'jerk' to his famous legal evasion
jokes.explanation: Mild political satire updating Clinton's famous quote for comedy context

jokes.text: He's Jimmy Carter with a Fox attitude!
jokes.punchline: Political comparison with network self-reference
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke referencing Fox's attitude while describing presidential comedian

jokes.text: It's true we're losing money. But there's no reason we can't dance, dance, dance the night away!
jokes.setup: Homer covering for dead Burns at investor meeting
jokes.punchline: Deflecting serious business concerns with dance suggestion
jokes.explanation: Homer's terrible crisis management turning business meeting into dance party

jokes.text: Those strings pulling me every which way jostled my heart from its slumber.
jokes.punchline: Puppet strings medically reviving his heart
jokes.explanation: Absurd medical explanation delivered in Burns' flowery language style

jokes.text: Oh, and you didn't? Well, the worm has finally shown its fangs!
jokes.punchline: Malapropism mixing 'worm has turned' with 'showing fangs'
jokes.explanation: Burns' characteristic mangled metaphor combining two different sayings

jokes.text: Smithers, make me slap him! / You call that a slap? Make me slap you. / Now, both. / Now, just you. / Now, give me a taste. / Now, both again. / Now, all three.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly complex slapping combinations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating sequence showing Burns' controlling nature through choreographed violence
